position_summary: Claude's final position is supported by the repository text: the four round-2 cleanups landed in v2.1, and I found no new inconsistency introduced by those edits. Sections 1-3 now read build-ready for FRMA prose: calibrated, internally self-consistent, and still pointed about the agentic triage loop, receipt-backed no, self-audit, and no-API driver.

dropped_findings.rationale: Addressed. The §3.2 funnel now says the referee keeps 'chain-held survivors (receipt-complete at every hop)' and reports '43 with a positive disease-hop enrichment → 30 clean full-chain nominations' at docs/manuscript/sections/03_methods.md:81-85. Search found no remaining 'disease-supported' or 'chain-supported' shorthand in sections 1-3.
Addressed. The label-shuffle control now reads 'which estimate the null disease-hop pass rate' at docs/manuscript/sections/01_introduction.md:67-70, not 'approach chance'.
Addressed. All three section headers read 'DRAFT v2' at docs/manuscript/sections/01_introduction.md:3, docs/manuscript/sections/02_background.md:3, and docs/manuscript/sections/03_methods.md:3.

Addressed. The network sentence now says approvals were for named endpoints and frames this as 'an auditable, endpoint-specific approval trail rather than a claim of an enforced allowlist' at docs/manuscript/sections/03_methods.md:137-140.
